    ALL AI Learning N Word·Idiom Meanings Examples EN-EN Dictionary

    Word·Idiom 5

    recalibration
    • US
    • GB

    • Noun 재교정

    국제언어대학원대학교 신어사전

    recalibration
    • US
    • GB

    Words gathered from web pages, some of inaccurate information may be included.

    • 재측정

    한국정보화진흥원(NIA), 한국전자통신연구원(ETRI)

    Intstrument Recalibration Date
    • US
    • GB

    • Unit Measures (Abbr.) IRD.

    Abbreviations.com

    frequent recalibration
    • US
    • GB

    Words gathered from web pages, some of inaccurate information may be included.

    • 빈번한 재보정

    한국정보화진흥원(NIA), 한국전자통신연구원(ETRI)

    recalibration of measuring apparatus
    • US
    • GB

    Words gathered from web pages, some of inaccurate information may be included.

    • 측정장치의 재교정업

    Web Collection

    Examples 46

    Example TTS Settings

    Click here to change the voice and speed of TTS!

    TTS Settings
    With Naver's voice synthesis technology,
    Naver English Dictionary provides natural-sounding and high-quality TTS voices.
    Voice
    Speed
    1st play
    0.6X 0.8X 0.9X 1X 1.2X
    2nd play
    0.6X 0.8X 0.9X 1X 1.2X

    Let's come up with a plan after the results of the recalibration.

    재측정 결과에 따라 대응 방안을 마련합시다.

      국제언어대학원대학교 신어사전
      Let there be no more nonsense about expressions of regret or recalibration of maritime borders, my friends.

        Web Collection

        He said poll workers are trained to perform the recalibration whenever a voter says the touch screen isn't sensitive enough.

          Web Collection

          That led to some recalibration of the work that those CSOs were doing, so that they are now much more focused on the poorest.
            Hansard
            There are issues there about recalibration of meters and hopefully we can touch on that, but just to reinforce that inequality.
              Hansard
              View more examples

              Meanings 1

              recalibrations
              • US
              • GB

              Words gathered from web pages, some of inaccurate information may be included.

              • [Noun] Plural form of recalibration
